name: Colin McRoberts
occupation: Associate Teaching Professor of Law, University of Kansas School of Business; attorney and negotiation consultant
education: B.A. in International Studies, Trinity University (San Antonio, TX), 2001; J.D., Harvard Law School, 2004
family: Married with children. Moved to Kansas with his wife to teach and start their family.
district: Kansas 1st Congressional District (the 'Big First') — covers 60+ counties across western and central Kansas, including Hays, Salina, Manhattan, Dodge City, Garden City, and (since 2022 redistricting) Lawrence. Cook PVI: R+16.
whoTheyAre:
Colin McRoberts is a lawyer, law professor, and negotiation consultant running as a Democrat for the Kansas 1st Congressional District. His full legal name is Kenneth Colin McRoberts. He earned his law degree from Harvard Law School in 2004 and began his career as a commercial litigator at Steptoe & Johnson LLP, where he secured multimillion-dollar wins for clients. He later founded his own consulting firm, Vasher McRoberts LLC, advising corporations, unions, diplomatic teams, and government institutions on negotiation and communication. He has worked with Fortune 500 companies, diplomats, and military special forces in dozens of countries.

Today McRoberts teaches business law, business fundamentals, and a course on the origin and spread of conspiracy theories at the University of Kansas School of Business. He also teaches negotiation to MBA students at the University of Missouri-Kansas City. He is a published author whose work has appeared in Aeon Magazine and Wired, and he writes the blog "Violent Metaphors." He has a book in progress called The Good Fight, about how irrational ideas capture rational people. In 2016, he attended a cruise-ship seminar for conspiracy theorists as research for his writing and was later interviewed by Skeptical Inquirer and Kansas Public Radio about the experience.

McRoberts was not born in Kansas but chose to move there with his wife. He lives in Lawrence, which was drawn into the 1st District during 2022 redistricting. He filed his campaign on September 17, 2025, making him the first Democrat to enter the KS-01 race.
theirRecord:
McRoberts has never held elected office. His public record is entirely professional: commercial litigation, international consulting, and university teaching. He is a first-time candidate in a district that has elected a Republican to Congress in every cycle since 1952, when Howard Miller was the last Democrat to win the seat.

His campaign centers on tariffs and trade, executive overreach, and bridging the cultural gap between eastern and western Kansas. He wants Congress to take back tariff authority from the executive branch, create a small-business exemption so family farms can import supplies with no reasonable domestic substitute, open foreign markets for American crops, and restore USAID's mission of buying American agricultural products. He has also spoken about protecting Social Security and Medicare, defending the rights of workers and teachers, and restoring Congressional oversight on trade policy.

Critics would point out that McRoberts has no governing experience and no track record of managing public budgets or casting legislative votes. He lives in Lawrence, the most liberal city in the district, which is culturally distinct from the western Kansas communities that make up most of KS-01. He has acknowledged he was not registered as a Democrat for years before running, which could raise questions about his partisan commitment among primary voters. His Harvard pedigree and academic focus on conspiracy theories, while intellectually serious, could become a liability in a deep-red district where those credentials may be framed as out of touch.
whatYouShouldKnow: McRoberts was directly inspired to run after attending Senator Roger Marshall's contentious town hall in Oakley, Kansas, on March 1, 2025. He drove four and a half hours from Lawrence to hear Marshall speak about Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. and vaccines, and he took one of the videos that went viral when Marshall was booed and left the event abruptly.
He is a Harvard Law graduate who practiced commercial litigation at Steptoe & Johnson LLP before founding his own international negotiation consulting firm.
He teaches a university course on the origin and spread of conspiracy theories and is writing a book called The Good Fight about how irrational ideas capture rational people.
His FEC filing through December 31, 2025, showed $24,699.29 in total receipts, $17,243.10 spent, and $7,456.19 cash on hand. That is a small federal campaign by Kansas congressional standards.
He was not registered as a Democrat for years before running, something he has openly acknowledged.

Both Democratic candidates in this race, McRoberts and Lauren Reinhold, live in Lawrence. No Democrat lives in the rural western half of the district that makes up the geographic and cultural core of KS-01.
He is affiliated with Take Back Congress, a Democratic candidate recruitment organization, and has spoken at the Kansas Democracy Fest in Hays.
Kansas 1st is rated R+16 by the Cook Political Report. Tracey Mann has won each of his three elections with roughly 70 percent of the vote. A Democrat has not won this seat since 1952.
whereTheyWorship: No church affiliation was found for Colin McRoberts in any of the public sources reviewed, including his campaign website, news interviews, social media profiles, and public records. His campaign materials do not reference faith, a congregation, or a denomination. If he attends a church, that information is not part of his public-facing profile as of the research date.

quotes.text: The long odds are exactly why this fight is so important. Gerrymandering and partisanship have kept our incumbent politicians safe even as they give up on representing Kansans in order to serve national donors and powerbrokers. This campaign is about giving Kansans the power to choose a better deal.

He does not care what that vote does. He doesn't care what it does to his neighbors. He doesn't care what it does to the rest of Kansas. He doesn't care what it does to the rest of the world, as long as it serves his interests, makes the party happy, gets him more qualified to one day go to the Senate or take some other easy, easy, no-fight election.

I think that's been going on for a long time, an intentional strategy to use the east against the west, and maybe to a lesser extent, the west against the east, to drive these wedges for the benefit of party politics. And I will not and cannot play that game. We're all neighbors. The 1st District is much more alike than it is different.

The first district is red in a red state. This is not going to be an easy race, it's not going to be a cheap race, it's not going to be a short race, but it's possible to win in a way it hasn't been before.
quotes.source: Kansas Democracy Fest in Hays (Hays Post)

quotes.text: I'm running not as a politician, but as a businessman and negotiator.
quotes.source: Kansas Democracy Fest in Hays (Hays Post)

campaignFinance.narrative: McRoberts's FEC filing through December 31, 2025, showed $24,699.29 in total receipts, with $17,243.10 spent and $7,456.19 cash on hand. The campaign is funded almost entirely by individual contributions, with $19,130.00 in itemized individual donations and $338.00 in candidate contributions. There are no PAC donations on record. This is a small federal campaign by Kansas congressional standards, but it represents an active operation rather than a placeholder filing. A richer donor ledger was not available beyond the FEC summary.
campaignFinance.undisclosed: No PAC contributions or dark money sources were identified. The remaining balance between total receipts and the itemized individual figure likely represents unitemized small-dollar donations below the FEC disclosure threshold.
